---
title: "Benchmarking TorchTextClassifiers at INSEE"
author: "Julien Bernardi"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: false
lang: en
execute:
  python: .venv/bin/python
  echo: false
keep-ipynb: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
  figcaption, .figure-caption { margin-top: 14px; }
</style>
```

## Introduction

This report presents the work carried out during an internship at **INSEE** (Institut national de la statistique et des études économiques), France's national statistics institute.

The internship focuses on **text classification**, a core natural language processing (NLP) task that consists in assigning a predefined category to a piece of text. At INSEE, a concrete use case is the automatic coding of business creation declarations: when a company is founded, its activity is described in free text by the declarant and must be assigned a code from the NAF (*Nomenclature des Activités Françaises*), a hierarchical classification of economic activities. A model has recently been deployed to automate this coding process, making it a concrete reference point for evaluating the lightweight architectures studied in this internship.

Large language models (LLMs) achieve strong performance on text classification, but they come with significant computational costs. In a production environment processing millions of records, lighter and faster alternatives are desirable. This internship investigates whether **lightweight neural architectures** — such as FastText-style mean-pooling models and small transformer encoders — can reach competitive accuracy at a fraction of the cost. All experiments are built on top of [**`torchTextClassifiers`**](https://github.com/InseeFrLab/torchTextClassifiers), a Python package developed at INSEE that provides a unified interface for training and evaluating these architectures.

The main objectives are:

1. Benchmark lightweight text classification models across several datasets.
2. Study the impact of key hyperparameters: embedding dimension, number of transformer layers, number of attention heads, vocabulary size, and learning rate.
3. Evaluate the trade-off between model size (number of parameters) and classification accuracy, through systematic validation loss curves.
4. Compare several architectures — from FastText to attention-based models — across different sequence aggregation strategies (mean pooling, label attention) to understand when the added complexity pays off.

## Benchmark Setup

### Datasets

The benchmark covers two publicly available sentiment analysis datasets of increasing difficulty.

**IMDb** (`stanfordnlp/imdb`) is the Large Movie Review Dataset [@maas2011]. It contains 25,000 training and 25,000 test English movie reviews, each labelled as positive or negative (reviews with a score ≥ 7/10 or ≤ 4/10 on IMDb). The binary nature of the task and the length of the reviews make it a standard NLP benchmark. SOTA accuracy reaches ~97% with large transformer ensembles; RoBERTa alone achieves ~95%.

**Amazon Reviews** (`amazon_reviews_multi`, English) is the Multilingual Amazon Reviews Corpus (MARC) introduced by @keung2020. The English split contains 200,000 training and 5,000 test product reviews, each rated from 1 to 5 stars. The input combines the review title and body. The 5-class ordinal nature of the task makes it harder than binary sentiment: the mBERT baseline from the original paper reaches 63.3% accuracy.

In our experiments, 2,000 (IMDb) and 5,000 (Amazon) training examples are held out as a validation set, leaving 23,000 and 195,000 examples for training respectively.

| Dataset | Classes | Train | Val | Test | SOTA (acc.) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IMDb | 2 | 23,000 | 2,000 | 5,000 | ~97% (ensemble) |
| Amazon reviews | 5 | 195,000 | 5,000 | 5,000 | 63.3% (mBERT) |

### Models

Four architectures of increasing complexity are compared, combining two model families with two sequence aggregation strategies:

- **FastText**: embeddings are averaged across tokens and fed directly to a classification head. No attention mechanism, minimal parameter count, very fast at both training and inference.
- **FastText + Label Attention**: same backbone as FastText, but the mean pool is replaced by a cross-attention step where one learned embedding per class acts as the query, allowing the model to focus on the tokens most relevant to each candidate class.
- **AttentiveFastText**: transformer encoder blocks (multi-head self-attention + feed-forward) are stacked on top of the embeddings before mean pooling, capturing contextual interactions between tokens.
- **AttentiveFastText + Label Attention**: same transformer backbone, but with label attention as the aggregation step instead of mean pooling.

```{=html}
<div style="margin:1.5rem 0;font-family:sans-serif;">

<!-- Architecture selector (2 rows) -->
<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:8px;margin-bottom:1.4rem;">
  <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;flex-wrap:wrap;">
    <span style="font-size:12px;color:#888;font-weight:600;min-width:90px;">Architecture</span>
    <button id="btn-fasttext"  onclick="switchModel('fasttext')"  style="padding:6px 14px;font-size:13px;font-weight:700;border-radius:20px;cursor:pointer;border:2px solid #1565C0;background:#1565C0;color:#fff;transition:all .25s;">FastText</button>
    <button id="btn-attentive" onclick="switchModel('attentive')" style="padding:6px 14px;font-size:13px;font-weight:700;border-radius:20px;cursor:pointer;border:2px solid #7b1fa2;background:#fff;color:#7b1fa2;transition:all .25s;">AttentiveFastText</button>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;flex-wrap:wrap;">
    <span style="font-size:12px;color:#888;font-weight:600;min-width:90px;">Aggregation</span>
    <button id="btn-mean"   onclick="switchAgg('mean')"   style="padding:6px 14px;font-size:13px;font-weight:700;border-radius:20px;cursor:pointer;border:2px solid #455a64;background:#fff;color:#455a64;transition:all .25s;">Mean Pooling</button>
    <button id="btn-labatt" onclick="switchAgg('labatt')" style="padding:6px 14px;font-size:13px;font-weight:700;border-radius:20px;cursor:pointer;border:2px solid #2e7d32;background:#fff;color:#2e7d32;transition:all .25s;">Label Attention</button>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Conteneur commun pour la transition -->
<div id="arch-container" style="position:relative;height:420px;transition:height .38s ease;">

<!-- FastText 2-row diagram -->
<div id="arch-fasttext-wrap"
  style="overflow:hidden;cursor:grab;position:absolute;top:0;left:0;right:0;height:420px;
         border-radius:8px;border:1px solid #eee;background:#f5f5f5;user-select:none;
         opacity:1;transform:translateX(0);pointer-events:auto;
         transition:opacity .38s ease,transform .38s ease;">
<div id="arch-fasttext" style="display:block;width:100%;padding:20px;box-sizing:border-box;
     position:absolute;">

  <!-- Grand carré englobant -->
  <div style="border:2px solid #d0d0d0;border-radius:14px;background:#fff;
              padding:24px 28px;width:100%;box-sizing:border-box;
              display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;gap:0;
              box-shadow:0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.06);">

    <!-- ── LIGNE 1 : Input → WordPiece → Embedding → σ → Mean Pool ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:nowrap;gap:0;">

      <!-- Input stub -->
      <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;margin-right:2px;">
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;">(B,)&nbsp;str</div>
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#bbb;">─────▶</div>
      </div>

      <!-- WordPiece -->
      <div id="ft-wp" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#78909c;background:#eceff1;"
        data-tip="The WordPiece tokenizer is trained on the training corpus. It splits raw text into sub-word pieces (e.g. 'playing' : 'play' + 'ing') and maps each to an integer ID. Output is truncated to seq_len tokens; shorter sequences are right-padded with padding_idx=0. An attention mask (1 = real token, 0 = pad) is produced alongside the IDs.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#546e7a;">⌨</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#37474f;">WordPiece</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq_len)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Embedding -->
      <div id="ft-emb" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#1976d2;background:#e3f2fd;"
        data-tip="nn.Embedding : a learnable look-up table of shape (vocab_size × emb_dim). Each token ID selects one row, replacing the integer with a dense float vector. Parameters: vocab_size × emb_dim (the largest component of FastText).">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#1565c0;">⊞</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#0d47a1;">Embedding</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#1976d2;">vocab_size × emb_dim</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq_len, emb_dim)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
      </div>

      <!-- σ RMSNorm token-level -->
      <div id="ft-rms1" class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="RMSNorm (per token): normalises each token embedding by its root-mean-square — x̂ = x / RMS(x) · γ — where γ ∈ ℝ<sup>emb_dim</sup> is a learnable scale vector (emb_dim params). Shape unchanged. Stabilises embedding magnitudes before pooling.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:72px;">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Mean Pool -->
      <div id="ft-pool" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#388e3c;background:#e8f5e9;"
        data-tip="The attention mask (1 / 0) is used to exclude padding positions. Real token embeddings are summed then divided by the count of real tokens, collapsing the seq_len dimension. No parameters. This is the core FastText design choice: word order is ignored. Produces a sentence embedding.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#2e7d32;">∑÷n</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#1b5e20;">Mean Pool</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#388e3c;">masked avg<br>over seq_len</div>
      </div>

    </div>

    <!-- ── CONNECTEUR : Ligne 1 → Ligne 2 ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;padding:10px 0 6px;">
      <div style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#666;white-space:nowrap;">(B, emb_dim)</div>
      <div style="font-size:28px;color:#bbb;line-height:1.1;">↓</div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── LIGNE 2 : σ (sentence) → Linear → Output ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:nowrap;gap:0;">

      <!-- σ RMSNorm sentence-level -->
      <div id="ft-rms2" class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="RMSNorm (per sentence): same operation applied to the pooled sentence vector. Normalises the aggregated representation before the linear head, preventing scale instability across batches. γ ∈ ℝ<sup>emb_dim</sup> (emb_dim params). Shape unchanged.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:72px;">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, emb_dim)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Linear -->
      <div id="ft-lin" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#f57c00;background:#fff3e0;"
        data-tip="W·x + b — a single linear layer mapping the emb_dim-dim sentence vector to n_classes logits. W ∈ ℝ<sup>n_classes×emb_dim</sup>, b ∈ ℝ<sup>n_classes</sup>. The argmax gives the predicted class.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#e65100;">⊸</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#bf360c;">Linear</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#f57c00;">emb_dim → n_classes</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Output stub -->
      <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;gap:14px;margin-left:2px;">
        <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;">
          <div style="font-size:9.5px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;">(B, n_classes)</div>
          <div style="font-size:13px;color:#bbb;">────▶</div>
        </div>
        <div style="font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;margin-top:10px;">ŷ (logits)</div>
      </div>

    </div>

  </div><!-- end grand carré -->

</div><!-- end diagram -->
</div><!-- end fasttext wrap -->

<!-- Transformer 3-row diagram -->
<div id="arch-transformer-wrap"
  style="overflow:hidden;cursor:grab;position:absolute;top:0;left:0;right:0;height:660px;
         border-radius:8px;border:1px solid #eee;background:#f5f5f5;user-select:none;
         opacity:0;transform:translateX(60px);pointer-events:none;
         transition:opacity .38s ease,transform .38s ease;">
<div id="arch-transformer" style="display:flex;justify-content:center;align-items:flex-start;
     width:100%;padding:20px;box-sizing:border-box;
     position:absolute;">

  <!-- Grand carré englobant -->
  <div style="border:2px solid #d0d0d0;border-radius:14px;background:#fff;
              padding:24px 28px;
              display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:flex-start;gap:0;
              box-shadow:0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.06);">

    <!-- ── LIGNE 1 : Input → WordPiece → Embedding → RMSNorm (token) ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:nowrap;gap:0;">

      <!-- Input stub -->
      <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;margin-right:2px;">
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;">(B,)&nbsp;str</div>
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#bbb;">─────▶</div>
      </div>

      <!-- WordPiece -->
      <div id="tr-wp" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#78909c;background:#eceff1;"
        data-tip="The WordPiece tokenizer is trained on the training corpus. It splits raw text into sub-word pieces (e.g. 'playing' : 'play' + 'ing') and maps each to an integer ID. Output is truncated to seq_len tokens; shorter sequences are right-padded with padding_idx=0. An attention mask (1 = real token, 0 = pad) is produced alongside the IDs.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#546e7a;">⌨</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#37474f;">WordPiece</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq_len)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">int64</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Embedding -->
      <div id="tr-emb" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#1976d2;background:#e3f2fd;"
        data-tip="nn.Embedding : a learnable look-up table of shape (vocab_size × emb_dim). Each token ID selects one row, replacing the integer with a dense float vector. Parameters: vocab_size × emb_dim (the largest component of FastText).">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#1565c0;">⊞</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#0d47a1;">Embedding</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#1976d2;">vocab_size × emb_dim</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq_len, emb_dim)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>

      <!-- σ RMSNorm token-level -->
      <div id="tr-rms1" class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="RMSNorm (per token): normalises each token embedding by its root-mean-square — x̂ = x / RMS(x) · γ — where γ ∈ ℝ<sup>emb_dim</sup> is a learnable scale vector (emb_dim params). Shape unchanged. Stabilises embedding magnitudes before pooling.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>

    </div>

    <!-- ── CONNECTEUR 1 : Ligne 1 → Ligne 2 ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;padding:10px 0 6px;">
      <div style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#666;white-space:nowrap;">(B, seq_len, emb_dim) · float32</div>
      <div style="font-size:28px;color:#bbb;line-height:1.1;">↓</div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── LIGNE 2 : Bloc Transformer (× n_layers) ── -->
    <div style="border:1.5px dashed #9c27b0;border-radius:10px;padding:14px 18px;
                position:relative;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:0;">

      <!-- Badge × n_layers -->
      <div style="position:absolute;top:-13px;left:50%;transform:translateX(-50%);
                  font-size:9.5px;color:#7b1fa2;font-weight:700;background:#fff;
                  padding:2px 10px;border:1px solid #ce93d8;border-radius:10px;white-space:nowrap;">
        × n_layers
      </div>

      <!-- σ pre-attn -->
      <div id="tr-prenorm-attn" class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;color:#4a148c;"
        data-tip="<b>Pre-attention RMSNorm</b><br>x_normed = x / RMS(x) — x is kept for the residual: x ← x + attn_out<br><i style='color:#888;'>Only active from block 2 onward: block 1's input already comes from the token-level RMSNorm, so applying this norm there would normalise twice in a row.</i>">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:56px;">
        <div class="ah-shape" style="font-size:8px;">(B, seq, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">pre-attn</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Multi-Head Attention -->
      <div id="tr-mha" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;min-width:148px;"
        data-tip="<b>Multi-Head Self-Attention</b><br>① <b>Project</b> — Q = xW<sub>Q</sub>, K = xW<sub>K</sub>, V = xW<sub>V</sub> · head_dim = emb_dim / n_head<br><i style='font-size:12px;color:#888;'>GQA: if n_kv_head &lt; n_head, K and V are shared across heads</i><br>② <b>RoPE</b> — rotate Q and K by angle θ·position to encode relative distance<br>③ <b>QK-norm</b> — normalise Q and K separately before the dot product<br>④ <b>Attend</b> — softmax(QK<sup>T</sup> / √head_dim) · V → (B, n_head, seq, head_dim)<br>⑤ <b>Project back</b> — reassemble heads + linear → (B, seq, emb_dim)<br>⑥ <b>Residual</b> — x ← x + attn_out">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#6a1b9a;font-style:italic;letter-spacing:3px;font-size:11px;">Q K V</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#4a148c;">Multi-Head Attn</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#7b1fa2;">head_dim = emb_dim / n_head</div>
      </div>

      <!-- +res + flèche vers pre-MLP -->
      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:62px;">
        <div class="ah-shape" style="color:#9c27b0;font-size:9px;font-weight:700;">↺ +res</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">(B, seq, emb)</div>
      </div>

      <!-- σ pre-MLP -->
      <div id="tr-prenorm-mlp" class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;color:#4a148c;"
        data-tip="Pre-MLP RMSNorm. x_normed = x / RMS(x). The value x is kept for the residual connection x ← x + mlp(x_normed). Shape unchanged.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:56px;">
        <div class="ah-shape" style="font-size:8px;">(B, seq, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">pre-MLP</div>
      </div>

      <!-- MLP -->
      <div id="tr-mlp" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;"
        data-tip="<b>Feed-Forward Network (MLP)</b><br>① <b>Expand</b> — Linear(emb_dim → 4·emb_dim)<br>② <b>Activate</b> — Squared ReLU: f(x) = ReLU(x)²<br>③ <b>Project back</b> — Linear(4·emb_dim → emb_dim)<br>④ <b>Residual</b> — x ← x + mlp_out">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#6a1b9a;">⇔</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#4a148c;">MLP</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#7b1fa2;">emb_dim → 4·emb_dim → emb_dim</div>
      </div>

      <!-- +res fin de bloc -->
      <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;padding:0 10px;">
        <div style="font-size:11px;color:#9c27b0;font-weight:700;white-space:nowrap;">↺ +res</div>
      </div>

    </div>

    <!-- ── CONNECTEUR 2 : Ligne 2 → Ligne 3 ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;padding:10px 0 6px;">
      <div style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#666;white-space:nowrap;">(B, seq_len, emb_dim) · float32</div>
      <div style="font-size:28px;color:#bbb;line-height:1.1;">↓</div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── LIGNE 3 : Post-norm → Mean Pool → Pre-head norm → Linear → Output ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:nowrap;gap:0;">

      <!-- σ post-blocks -->
      <div id="tr-postnorm" class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="Post-blocks RMSNorm. Applied after the last transformer block. Normalises before pooling. Shape (B, seq_len, emb_dim) unchanged.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:70px;">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Mean Pool -->
      <div id="tr-pool" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#388e3c;background:#e8f5e9;"
        data-tip="Masked mean pooling. Averages token embeddings over seq_len, excluding padding positions. (B, seq_len, emb_dim) → (B, emb_dim). Same operation as FastText but applied to contextualised vectors.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#2e7d32;">∑÷n</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#1b5e20;">Mean Pool</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#388e3c;">(B, seq, emb_dim)<br>→ (B, emb_dim)</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, emb_dim)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>

      <!-- σ pre-head -->
      <div id="tr-headnorm" class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="RMSNorm (per sentence): same operation applied to the pooled sentence vector. Normalises the aggregated representation before the linear head, preventing scale instability across batches. γ ∈ ℝ<sup>emb_dim</sup> (emb_dim params). Shape unchanged.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>

      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:70px;">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, emb_dim)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Linear -->
      <div id="tr-lin" class="ah-block" style="border-color:#f57c00;background:#fff3e0;"
        data-tip="W·x + b — a single linear layer mapping the emb_dim-dim sentence vector to n_classes logits. W ∈ ℝ<sup>n_classes×emb_dim</sup>, b ∈ ℝ<sup>n_classes</sup>. The argmax gives the predicted class.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#e65100;">⊸</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#bf360c;">Linear</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#f57c00;">emb_dim → n_classes</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Output stub -->
      <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;gap:14px;margin-left:2px;">
        <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;">
          <div style="font-size:9.5px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;">(B, n_classes)</div>
          <div style="font-size:13px;color:#bbb;">────▶</div>
        </div>
        <div style="font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;margin-top:10px;">ŷ (logits)</div>
      </div>

    </div>

  </div><!-- end grand carré -->

</div><!-- end transformer diagram -->
</div><!-- end transformer wrap -->

<!-- Label Attention 3-row diagram -->
<div id="arch-labatt-wrap"
  style="overflow:hidden;cursor:grab;position:absolute;top:0;left:0;right:0;height:660px;
         border-radius:8px;border:1px solid #eee;background:#f5f5f5;user-select:none;
         opacity:0;transform:translateX(60px);pointer-events:none;
         transition:opacity .38s ease,transform .38s ease;">
<div id="arch-labatt" style="display:flex;justify-content:center;align-items:flex-start;
     width:100%;padding:20px;box-sizing:border-box;
     position:absolute;">

  <div style="border:2px solid #d0d0d0;border-radius:14px;background:#fff;
              padding:24px 28px;
              display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:flex-start;gap:0;
              box-shadow:0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.06);">

    <!-- ── ROW 1 : Input → WordPiece → Embedding → RMSNorm ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:nowrap;gap:0;">
      <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;margin-right:2px;">
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;">(B,)&nbsp;str</div>
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#bbb;">─────▶</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-block" style="border-color:#78909c;background:#eceff1;"
        data-tip="The WordPiece tokenizer is trained on the training corpus. It splits raw text into sub-word pieces (e.g. 'playing' : 'play' + 'ing') and maps each to an integer ID. Output is truncated to seq_len tokens; shorter sequences are right-padded with padding_idx=0. An attention mask (1 = real token, 0 = pad) is produced alongside the IDs.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#546e7a;">⌨</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#37474f;">WordPiece</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq_len)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">int64</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-block" style="border-color:#1976d2;background:#e3f2fd;"
        data-tip="nn.Embedding : a learnable look-up table of shape (vocab_size × emb_dim). Each token ID selects one row, replacing the integer with a dense float vector. Parameters: vocab_size × emb_dim (the largest component of FastText).">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#1565c0;">⊞</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#0d47a1;">Embedding</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#1976d2;">vocab_size × emb_dim</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq_len, emb_dim)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="RMSNorm (per token): normalises each token embedding by its root-mean-square — x̂ = x / RMS(x) · γ — where γ ∈ ℝ<sup>emb_dim</sup> is a learnable scale vector (emb_dim params). Shape unchanged. Stabilises embedding magnitudes before pooling.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── CONNECTOR 1 ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;padding:10px 0 6px;">
      <div style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#666;white-space:nowrap;">(B, seq_len, emb_dim) · float32</div>
      <div style="font-size:28px;color:#bbb;line-height:1.1;">↓</div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── ROW 2 : Transformer Block × n_layers ── -->
    <div style="border:1.5px dashed #9c27b0;border-radius:10px;padding:14px 18px;
                position:relative;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:0;">
      <div style="position:absolute;top:-13px;left:50%;transform:translateX(-50%);
                  font-size:9.5px;color:#7b1fa2;font-weight:700;background:#fff;
                  padding:2px 10px;border:1px solid #ce93d8;border-radius:10px;white-space:nowrap;">
        × n_layers
      </div>
      <div class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;color:#4a148c;"
        data-tip="<b>Pre-attention RMSNorm</b><br>x_normed = x / RMS(x) — x is kept for the residual: x ← x + attn_out<br><i style='color:#888;'>Only active from block 2 onward: block 1's input already comes from the token-level RMSNorm, so applying this norm there would normalise twice in a row.</i>">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:56px;">
        <div class="ah-shape" style="font-size:8px;">(B, seq, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">pre-attn</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-block" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;min-width:148px;"
        data-tip="<b>Multi-Head Self-Attention</b><br>① <b>Project</b> — Q = xW<sub>Q</sub>, K = xW<sub>K</sub>, V = xW<sub>V</sub> · head_dim = emb_dim / n_head<br><i style='font-size:12px;color:#888;'>GQA: if n_kv_head &lt; n_head, K and V are shared across heads</i><br>② <b>RoPE</b> — rotate Q and K by angle θ·position to encode relative distance<br>③ <b>QK-norm</b> — normalise Q and K separately before the dot product<br>④ <b>Attend</b> — softmax(QK<sup>T</sup> / √head_dim) · V → (B, n_head, seq, head_dim)<br>⑤ <b>Project back</b> — reassemble heads + linear → (B, seq, emb_dim)<br>⑥ <b>Residual</b> — x ← x + attn_out">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#6a1b9a;font-style:italic;letter-spacing:3px;font-size:11px;">Q K V</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#4a148c;">Multi-Head Attn</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#7b1fa2;">head_dim = emb_dim / n_head</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:62px;">
        <div class="ah-shape" style="color:#9c27b0;font-size:9px;font-weight:700;">↺ +res</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">(B, seq, emb)</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;color:#4a148c;"
        data-tip="Pre-MLP RMSNorm. x_normed = x / RMS(x). The value x is kept for the residual connection x ← x + mlp(x_normed). Shape unchanged.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:56px;">
        <div class="ah-shape" style="font-size:8px;">(B, seq, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">pre-MLP</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-block" style="border-color:#6a1b9a;background:#f3e5f5;"
        data-tip="<b>Feed-Forward Network (MLP)</b><br>① <b>Expand</b> — Linear(emb_dim → 4·emb_dim)<br>② <b>Activate</b> — Squared ReLU: f(x) = ReLU(x)²<br>③ <b>Project back</b> — Linear(4·emb_dim → emb_dim)<br>④ <b>Residual</b> — x ← x + mlp_out">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#6a1b9a;">⇔</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#4a148c;">MLP</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#7b1fa2;">emb_dim → 4·emb_dim → emb_dim</div>
      </div>
      <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;padding:0 10px;">
        <div style="font-size:11px;color:#9c27b0;font-weight:700;white-space:nowrap;">↺ +res</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── CONNECTOR 2 ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;padding:10px 0 6px;">
      <div style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#666;white-space:nowrap;">(B, seq_len, emb_dim) · float32</div>
      <div style="font-size:28px;color:#bbb;line-height:1.1;">↓</div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── ROW 3 : post-norm → Label Attention → pre-head norm → Linear(d→1) → output ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:nowrap;gap:0;">
      <div class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="Post-blocks RMSNorm. Applied after the last transformer block. Normalises before label attention. Shape (B, seq_len, emb_dim) unchanged.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:70px;">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, seq, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-block" style="border-color:#2e7d32;background:#e8f5e9;min-width:148px;"
        data-tip="<b>Label Attention (cross-attention)</b><br>One learned embedding per class acts as the query; token embeddings are keys and values.<br>① <b>Label embeds</b> — nn.Embedding(n_classes, emb_dim): one trainable vector per class → Q: (B, n_classes, emb_dim)<br>② <b>Project</b> — Q = label_embeds·W<sub>Q</sub>, K = tokens·W<sub>K</sub>, V = tokens·W<sub>V</sub> · head_dim = emb_dim / n_head<br>③ <b>QK-norm</b> — normalise Q and K before the dot product<br>④ <b>Attend</b> — softmax(QK<sup>T</sup> / √head_dim) · V, padding positions masked → (B, n_head, n_classes, head_dim)<br>⑤ <b>Project back</b> — reassemble heads + linear → (B, n_classes, emb_dim)<br>Each class gets its own sentence representation focused on its most relevant tokens.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#2e7d32;font-style:italic;letter-spacing:1px;font-size:10px;">L·Q  K  V</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#1b5e20;">Label Attention</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#388e3c;">labels → queries<br>tokens → keys/vals</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, n_classes, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-slim" style="border-color:#0097a7;background:#e0f7fa;color:#006064;"
        data-tip="RMSNorm applied to each of the n_classes label-specific sentence vectors before the classification head. Shape (B, n_classes, emb_dim) unchanged.">
        <div class="ah-slim-text">σ</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-arr" style="min-width:70px;">
        <div class="ah-shape">(B, n_cls, emb)</div>
        <div class="ah-line">────▶</div>
        <div class="ah-dtype">float32</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ah-block" style="border-color:#f57c00;background:#fff3e0;"
        data-tip="Linear(emb_dim → 1) applied independently to each of the n_classes label-specific vectors. W ∈ ℝ<sup>1×emb_dim</sup>. After squeeze: (B, n_classes) logits. Unlike FastText / Transformer where a single W maps all classes at once, here each class score comes from its own dedicated text representation.">
        <div class="ah-icon" style="color:#e65100;">⊸</div>
        <div class="ah-name" style="color:#bf360c;">Linear</div>
        <div class="ah-params" style="color:#f57c00;">emb_dim → 1<br>per class</div>
      </div>
      <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0;gap:14px;margin-left:2px;">
        <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;">
          <div style="font-size:9.5px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;">(B, n_classes)</div>
          <div style="font-size:13px;color:#bbb;">────▶</div>
        </div>
        <div style="font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:#333;white-space:nowrap;margin-top:10px;">ŷ (logits)</div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div><!-- end grand carré -->

</div><!-- end label attention diagram -->
</div><!-- end label attention wrap -->

</div><!-- end conteneur commun -->

<div style="font-size:10px;color:#aaa;margin-top:4px;text-align:center;">
  scroll to zoom · drag to pan · double-click to reset
</div>

<!-- Tooltip box -->
<div id="arch-tooltip-h"
  style="margin-top:.8rem;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;padding:12px 18px;background:#fff;border:2px solid #ddd;
         border-radius:6px;font-size:14px;color:#444;line-height:1.6;
         max-width:640px;display:none;text-align:center;
         transition:background .2s ease,border-color .2s ease;">
</div>

</div><!-- end outer -->

<style>
.ah-block {
  min-width:110px; max-width:130px; height:130px; padding:12px 10px; border-radius:10px;
  border:2px solid; text-align:center; cursor:default; flex-shrink:0;
  display:flex; flex-direction:column; justify-content:center; align-items:center;
  transition:box-shadow .15s,transform .15s;
}
.ah-block:hover { box-shadow:0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,.18); transform:translateY(-2px); }
.ah-icon   { font-size:20px; margin-bottom:4px; }
.ah-name   { font-size:13px; font-weight:700; margin-bottom:5px; }
.ah-params { font-size:10.5px; line-height:1.5; }
.ah-arr {
  display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center;
  flex-shrink:0; padding:0 3px; min-width:80px;
}
.ah-shape  { font-size:9.5px; font-weight:700; color:#333; white-space:nowrap; margin-bottom:1px; }
.ah-line   { font-size:14px; color:#bbb; letter-spacing:-2px; }
.ah-dtype  { font-size:9px; color:#aaa; margin-top:1px; }
.ah-slim {
  width:36px; height:130px; padding:12px 4px; border-radius:8px; border:2px solid;
  text-align:center; cursor:default; flex-shrink:0;
  display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center;
  transition:box-shadow .15s, transform .15s;
}
.ah-slim:hover { box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,.18); transform:translateY(-2px); }
.ah-slim-text { font-size:22px; font-weight:400; line-height:1; }
.ah-tblock {
  min-width:185px; max-width:205px; height:160px; padding:8px 10px; border-radius:10px;
  border:2px dashed #7b1fa2; background:#f9f0ff; flex-shrink:0;
  display:flex; flex-direction:column; justify-content:space-between;
  cursor:default; position:relative;
  transition:box-shadow .15s, transform .15s;
}
.ah-tblock:hover { box-shadow:0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,.18); transform:translateY(-2px); }
.ah-tblock-badge {
  position:absolute; top:-11px; right:8px;
  background:#7b1fa2; color:white; font-size:9px; font-weight:700;
  padding:2px 7px; border-radius:10px; white-space:nowrap;
}
.ah-tblock-section {
  background:#fff; border:1px solid #ce93d8; border-radius:6px;
  padding:5px 8px; flex:1;
  display:flex; flex-direction:column; justify-content:center;
}
.ah-tblock-title { font-size:11px; font-weight:700; color:#6a1b9a; margin-bottom:3px; }
.ah-tblock-dim   { font-size:8px; color:#8e24aa; line-height:1.5; }
</style>

<script>
(function(){
  var tip = document.getElementById('arch-tooltip-h');
  document.querySelectorAll('.ah-block, .ah-slim, .ah-tblock').forEach(function(el){
    el.addEventListener('mouseenter', function(){
      tip.innerHTML = el.getAttribute('data-tip');
      tip.style.display = 'block';
      var bg  = el.style.background || el.style.backgroundColor || '#fff';
      var bdr = el.style.borderColor || '#ddd';
      tip.style.background   = bg;
      tip.style.borderColor  = bdr;
    });
    el.addEventListener('mouseleave', function(){
      tip.style.display = 'none';
    });
  });

  var _curModel = 'fasttext', _curAgg = 'mean';

  function _applySelection(){
    var arch = (_curModel === 'fasttext') ? 'fasttext' : (_curAgg === 'labatt' ? 'labatt' : 'transformer');
    var ft   = document.getElementById('arch-fasttext-wrap');
    var tr   = document.getElementById('arch-transformer-wrap');
    var la   = document.getElementById('arch-labatt-wrap');
    var cont = document.getElementById('arch-container');
    var BASE = 'padding:6px 14px;font-size:13px;font-weight:700;border-radius:20px;cursor:pointer;transition:all .25s;border:2px solid ';

    ft.style.opacity='0'; ft.style.transform='translateX(-50px)'; ft.style.pointerEvents='none';
    tr.style.opacity='0'; tr.style.transform='translateX(-50px)'; tr.style.pointerEvents='none';
    la.style.opacity='0'; la.style.transform='translateX(-50px)'; la.style.pointerEvents='none';

    var target = arch==='fasttext' ? ft : arch==='labatt' ? la : tr;
    target.style.opacity='1'; target.style.transform='translateX(0)'; target.style.pointerEvents='auto';
    cont.style.height = arch==='fasttext' ? '420px' : '680px';

    document.getElementById('btn-fasttext').style.cssText  = BASE + (_curModel==='fasttext'  ? '#1565C0;background:#1565C0;color:#fff;' : '#1565C0;background:#fff;color:#1565C0;');
    document.getElementById('btn-attentive').style.cssText = BASE + (_curModel==='attentive' ? '#7b1fa2;background:#7b1fa2;color:#fff;' : '#7b1fa2;background:#fff;color:#7b1fa2;');

    var aggDisabled = _curModel === 'fasttext';
    document.getElementById('btn-mean').style.cssText   = aggDisabled ? BASE + '#bbb;background:#f5f5f5;color:#bbb;cursor:default;'
      : BASE + (_curAgg==='mean'   ? '#455a64;background:#455a64;color:#fff;' : '#455a64;background:#fff;color:#455a64;');
    document.getElementById('btn-labatt').style.cssText = aggDisabled ? BASE + '#bbb;background:#f5f5f5;color:#bbb;cursor:default;'
      : BASE + (_curAgg==='labatt' ? '#2e7d32;background:#2e7d32;color:#fff;' : '#2e7d32;background:#fff;color:#2e7d32;');
  }

  window.switchModel = function(m){ _curModel = m; _applySelection(); };
  window.switchAgg   = function(a){ if(_curModel !== 'fasttext'){ _curAgg = a; _applySelection(); } };

  // ── Pan + Zoom (générique) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  function makePanZoom(wrapId, diagId){
    var wrap = document.getElementById(wrapId);
    var diag = document.getElementById(diagId);
    if(!wrap || !diag) return;
    var sc=1, tx=0, ty=0, dragging=false, ox, oy, otx, oty;
    function applyT(){
      diag.style.transform='translate('+tx+'px,'+ty+'px) scale('+sc+')';
      diag.style.transformOrigin='0 0';
    }
    wrap.addEventListener('wheel',function(e){
      e.preventDefault();
      var r=wrap.getBoundingClientRect();
      var mx=e.clientX-r.left, my=e.clientY-r.top;
      var d=e.deltaY>0?0.88:1.12;
      tx=mx-(mx-tx)*d; ty=my-(my-ty)*d;
      sc=Math.max(0.25,Math.min(4,sc*d)); applyT();
    },{passive:false});
    wrap.addEventListener('mousedown',function(e){
      dragging=true; ox=e.clientX; oy=e.clientY; otx=tx; oty=ty;
      wrap.style.cursor='grabbing';
    });
    document.addEventListener('mousemove',function(e){
      if(!dragging) return;
      tx=otx+(e.clientX-ox); ty=oty+(e.clientY-oy); applyT();
    });
    document.addEventListener('mouseup',function(){
      dragging=false; wrap.style.cursor='grab';
    });
    wrap.addEventListener('dblclick',function(){ sc=1;tx=0;ty=0;applyT(); });
  }
  makePanZoom('arch-fasttext-wrap',    'arch-fasttext');
  makePanZoom('arch-transformer-wrap', 'arch-transformer');
  makePanZoom('arch-labatt-wrap',      'arch-labatt');
})();
</script>
```

All three architectures use a **WordPiece tokenizer** trained on the training data, with vocabulary sizes ranging from 2,000 to 20,000 tokens.

### Experimental Setup

All experiments are tracked with **MLflow** and run on GPU via **Argo Workflows** on the INSEE datalab infrastructure. Each configuration is trained for up to 40 epochs with early stopping (patience = 5).

The grid search covers 624 configurations in total. For FastText (`n_layers=0`), `n_head` and label attention are not applicable, yielding $4 \times 4 \times 3 = 48$ runs. For transformer architectures (`n_layers` ∈ {1, 2}), the full grid gives $4 \times 2 \times 3 \times 2 \times 4 \times 3 = 576$ runs.

| Hyperparameter | Values | Applies to |
|---|---|---|
| Embedding dimension | 64, 128, 256, 512 | All |
| Number of layers (`n_layers`) | 0, 1, 2 | All |
| Vocabulary size | 2,000 / 5,000 / 10,000 / 20,000 | All |
| Learning rate | 0.0001, 0.0005, 0.001 | All |
| Attention heads (`n_head`) | 2, 4, 8 | `n_layers` ≥ 1 |
| Label attention (`n_heads_label_attention`) | none, 4 | `n_layers` ≥ 1 |

## Experimental Study

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Amazon

```{python}
#| label: fetch-mlflow-amazon

from pathlib import Path
import mlflow
import pandas as pd

CACHE_PATH = Path("benchmark/results/amazon_runs.csv")
CACHE_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

if CACHE_PATH.exists():
    df = pd.read_csv(CACHE_PATH)
    if "metrics.train_time_s" in df.columns:
        df = df.rename(columns={"metrics.train_time_s": "train_time_s"})
else:
    df = mlflow.search_runs(experiment_names=["amazon"], max_results=2000)
    df = df[df["status"] == "FINISHED"].copy()
    df = df.rename(columns={
        "params.emb_dim":                  "emb_dim",
        "params.n_layers":                 "n_layers",
        "params.n_head":                   "n_head",
        "params.n_heads_label_attention":  "n_heads_label_attn",
        "params.vocab_size":               "vocab_size",
        "params.lr":                       "lr",
        "metrics.num_params":              "num_params",
        "metrics.test_accuracy":           "test_accuracy",
        "metrics.test_f1_macro":           "test_f1_macro",
        "metrics.train_time_s":            "train_time_s",
    })

    client = mlflow.MlflowClient()
    def get_min_val_loss(run_id):
        history = client.get_metric_history(run_id, "val_loss")
        return min(m.value for m in history) if history else None

    df["min_val_loss"] = df["run_id"].apply(get_min_val_loss)

    for col in ["emb_dim", "n_layers", "vocab_size"]:
        df[col] = df[col].astype(int)
    df["lr"] = df["lr"].astype(float)
    df["num_params"] = df["num_params"].astype(int)

    def arch_label(row):
        if row["n_layers"] == 0:
            return "FastText"
        elif str(row["n_heads_label_attn"]) == "4":
            return "Label Attention"
        else:
            return f"Transformer (L={row['n_layers']})"

    df["architecture"] = df.apply(arch_label, axis=1)
    df.to_csv(CACHE_PATH, index=False)
```

### Validation loss vs. number of parameters & stability

A key observation is that **`n_head` and `lr` do not affect `num_params`**. In a standard transformer, the total size of the attention matrices is $4 \times d^2$ regardless of how many heads are used, since $d_{\text{head}} = d / n_{\text{head}}$. Similarly, `lr` is a training hyperparameter with no effect on architecture. As a result, for any fixed combination of `(emb_dim, n_layers, vocab_size)`, there are exactly $3 \times 3 = 9$ runs sharing the same `num_params` (3 values of `n_head` × 3 values of `lr`).

This motivates two complementary views on the first tab:

- **Scatter plot**: all 624 runs, showing the full spread of achievable val_loss for a given parameter budget.
- **Curve plot**: for each `(architecture, emb_dim, vocab_size)` group, take the `min(val_loss)` over `n_head` and `lr`, collapsing the 9 runs into a single best-case point. Sorting by `num_params` then yields one clean curve per architecture, tracing the performance frontier as model capacity grows.

The second tab shows the **distribution of validation loss across all runs** for each architecture. A narrow violin indicates that the architecture is robust to hyperparameter choice, while a wide one signals high sensitivity.

::: {.panel-tabset}
### Val loss vs. parameters

```{python}
#| label: fig-pareto
#| fig-cap: "Validation loss vs. number of parameters"

import plotly.graph_objects as go

COLORS = {
    "FastText":           "#2196F3",
    "Transformer (L=1)": "#FF9800",
    "Transformer (L=2)": "#F44336",
    "Label Attention":   "#4CAF50",
}

df_curves = (
    df.groupby(["architecture", "emb_dim", "vocab_size", "num_params"], as_index=False)
    ["min_val_loss"].min()
    .sort_values("num_params")
)

df_ft      = df[df["architecture"] == "FastText"].groupby(
    ["architecture", "emb_dim", "vocab_size", "lr", "num_params"], as_index=False
)["min_val_loss"].min()
df_scatter = pd.concat([df_ft, df[df["architecture"] != "FastText"]], ignore_index=True)

fig = go.Figure()

for arch, group in df_scatter.groupby("architecture"):
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["num_params"], y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="markers",
        name=arch, legendgroup=arch,
        marker=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), size=5, opacity=0.3),
        hovertemplate="params=%{x:,}<br>val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
    ))

for arch, group in df_curves.groupby("architecture"):
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["num_params"], y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name=arch, legendgroup=arch, showlegend=False,
        line=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5),
        hovertemplate="params=%{x:,}<br>best val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Number of parameters",
    yaxis_title="Validation loss",
    legend_title="Architecture",
    height=450,
    xaxis=dict(tickformat=".2s"),
)
fig.show()
```

### Effect of training set size

```{python}
#| label: fig-train-eff
#| fig-cap: "Min. validation loss vs. number of parameters, for varying training set fractions. Dot size and opacity are proportional to the fraction of training data used."

from pathlib import Path

CACHE_PATH_EFF = Path("benchmark/results/amazon_train_eff_cache.csv")

if CACHE_PATH_EFF.exists():
    df_eff = pd.read_csv(CACHE_PATH_EFF)
else:
    df_eff = mlflow.search_runs(experiment_names=["amazon_train_eff"], max_results=2000)
    df_eff = df_eff[df_eff["status"] == "FINISHED"].copy()
    df_eff = df_eff.rename(columns={
        "params.train_fraction":           "train_fraction",
        "params.emb_dim":                  "emb_dim",
        "params.n_layers":                 "n_layers",
        "params.n_heads_label_attention":  "n_heads_label_attn",
        "metrics.num_params":              "num_params",
    })

    client = mlflow.MlflowClient()
    def _min_val_eff(run_id):
        h = client.get_metric_history(run_id, "val_loss")
        return min(m.value for m in h) if h else None

    df_eff["min_val_loss"] = df_eff["run_id"].apply(_min_val_eff)

    for col in ["emb_dim", "n_layers", "num_params"]:
        df_eff[col] = pd.to_numeric(df_eff[col], errors="coerce")
    df_eff["train_fraction"] = df_eff["train_fraction"].astype(float)

    def _arch_eff(row):
        if row["n_layers"] == 0:
            return "FastText"
        elif str(row["n_heads_label_attn"]) == "4":
            return "Label Attention"
        return f"Transformer (L={int(row['n_layers'])})"
    df_eff["architecture"] = df_eff.apply(_arch_eff, axis=1)
    df_eff.to_csv(CACHE_PATH_EFF, index=False)

df_eff_c = (
    df_eff.dropna(subset=["min_val_loss", "num_params"])
    .groupby(["architecture", "train_fraction", "num_params"], as_index=False)["min_val_loss"].min()
    .sort_values(["architecture", "train_fraction", "num_params"])
)

FRAC_SIZES   = {0.1: 7, 0.2: 10, 0.5: 14, 1.0: 19}
FRAC_OPACITY = {0.1: 0.40, 0.2: 0.55, 0.5: 0.75, 1.0: 1.0}
fractions    = sorted(df_eff_c["train_fraction"].unique())

fig = go.Figure()

# Lines per train_fraction connecting all architectures sorted by num_params
for frac, grp_line in df_eff_c.groupby("train_fraction"):
    grp_line = grp_line.sort_values("num_params")
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp_line["num_params"],
        y=grp_line["min_val_loss"],
        mode="lines",
        showlegend=False,
        line=dict(color="grey", width=1.2, dash="dot"),
        opacity=FRAC_OPACITY.get(frac, 0.6),
        hoverinfo="skip",
    ))

# Dots per (architecture, train_fraction)
seen_arch = set()
for (arch, frac), grp in df_eff_c.groupby(["architecture", "train_fraction"]):
    color = COLORS.get(arch, "grey")
    show  = arch not in seen_arch
    seen_arch.add(arch)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["num_params"],
        y=grp["min_val_loss"],
        mode="markers",
        name=arch,
        legendgroup=arch,
        showlegend=show,
        marker=dict(size=FRAC_SIZES.get(frac, 9), color=color, opacity=FRAC_OPACITY.get(frac, 0.7)),
        hovertemplate=(
            f"<b>{arch}</b><br>"
            f"train fraction: {frac}<br>"
            "params=%{x:,}<br>val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra></extra>"
        ),
    ))

for frac in fractions:
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=[None], y=[None],
        mode="markers",
        name=f"{int(frac * 100)}% of train set",
        legendgroup=f"frac_{frac}",
        marker=dict(size=FRAC_SIZES.get(frac, 9), color="grey", opacity=FRAC_OPACITY.get(frac, 0.7)),
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Number of parameters",
    yaxis_title="Min. validation loss",
    legend_title="Architecture / Train set",
    height=450,
    xaxis=dict(type="log", tickformat=".2s"),
)
fig.show()
```

### Stability

```{python}
#| label: fig-violin
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of validation loss by architecture"

arch_order = ["FastText", "Transformer (L=1)", "Transformer (L=2)", "Label Attention"]
arch_order = [a for a in arch_order if a in df["architecture"].unique()]

fig = go.Figure()
for arch in arch_order:
    color = COLORS.get(arch, "grey")
    vals  = df[df["architecture"] == arch]["min_val_loss"].dropna()
    # Violin
    fig.add_trace(go.Violin(
        y=vals, name=arch,
        side="both",
        fillcolor=color, opacity=0.25,
        line=dict(color=color, width=1.5),
        box_visible=False, meanline_visible=False,
        points=False,
        showlegend=False,
        hoverinfo="skip",
    ))
    # Box slim on top
    fig.add_trace(go.Box(
        y=vals, name=arch,
        marker=dict(color=color, size=3, opacity=0.4),
        line=dict(color=color, width=1.5),
        fillcolor="white",
        boxmean="sd",
        width=0.12,
        showlegend=False,
        hovertemplate="<b>" + arch + "</b><br>median=%{median:.4f}<br>Q1=%{q1:.4f}<br>Q3=%{q3:.4f}<extra></extra>",
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    template="simple_white",
    yaxis_title="Validation loss",
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e8e8e8"),
    showlegend=False,
    height=430,
    violingap=0.4,
    boxgap=0.4,
)
fig.show()
```

:::

**Val loss vs. parameters.** FastText plateaus around 0.94 regardless of model size, showing that averaging embeddings without any attention mechanism hits a hard performance ceiling. The two transformer variants improve steadily with capacity: Transformer (L=2) reaches the lowest validation loss (~0.87) and remains the best architecture across all parameter budgets. Transformer (L=1) sits in between, around 0.89–0.90. Label Attention is noisier — its curve oscillates more than the others, suggesting higher sensitivity to the specific hyperparameter combination.

**Effect of training set size.** Each dot represents the best validation loss achieved by a given architecture at a given model size, trained on a fraction of the full training set. Dot size and opacity both increase with the fraction used (10 % → 100 %). Across all architectures, moving from 10 % to 100 % of the training data consistently lowers validation loss, confirming that data volume matters even at these model scales. The gap between fractions is largest for the most capable architectures (Transformer L=2), suggesting that bigger models benefit more from additional data.

**Stability.** FastText is by far the most stable architecture: its violin is narrow and concentrated around 0.94–0.95, meaning the choice of hyperparameters has little impact on the outcome. Transformer (L=2) achieves the best median performance but at the cost of a wide distribution (~0.86–0.91), indicating that some configurations work much better than others. Label Attention shows a similar spread to Transformer (L=2) but with a slightly higher median, making it both sensitive and less performant on average.

### Effect of vocabulary size and embedding dimension

::: {.panel-tabset}
### Vocabulary size

```{python}
#| label: fig-vocab
#| fig-cap: "Effect of vocabulary size on validation loss"

import numpy as np

vocab_vals = sorted(df["vocab_size"].unique())
emb_vals   = sorted(df["emb_dim"].unique())

df_vocab_mean = (
    df.groupby(["architecture", "vocab_size"], as_index=False)["min_val_loss"].mean()
    .sort_values("vocab_size")
)
df_emb_mean = (
    df.groupby(["architecture", "emb_dim"], as_index=False)["min_val_loss"].mean()
    .sort_values("emb_dim")
)

vocab_offsets = {
    "FastText":           0,
    "Transformer (L=1)": -250,
    "Transformer (L=2)": +250,
    "Label Attention":   0,
}

fig = go.Figure()
for arch, group in df.groupby("architecture"):
    offset = vocab_offsets[arch]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["vocab_size"] + offset, y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="markers", name=arch, legendgroup=arch,
        marker=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), size=5, opacity=0.3),
        hovertemplate="vocab=%{customdata:,}<br>val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
        customdata=group["vocab_size"],
    ))
for arch, group in df_vocab_mean.groupby("architecture"):
    offset = vocab_offsets.get(arch, 0)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["vocab_size"] + offset, y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="lines+markers", name=arch, legendgroup=arch, showlegend=False,
        line=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), width=2), marker=dict(size=6),
        hovertemplate="vocab=%{customdata:,}<br>mean val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
        customdata=group["vocab_size"],
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Vocabulary size", yaxis_title="Validation loss",
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=vocab_vals, ticktext=[f"{v:,}" for v in vocab_vals]),
    legend_title="Architecture", height=430, width=750, margin=dict(l=60, r=20),
)
fig.show()
```

### Embedding dimension

```{python}
#| label: fig-emb
#| fig-cap: "Effect of embedding dimension on validation loss"

emb_offsets = {
    "FastText":           0,
    "Transformer (L=1)": -6,
    "Transformer (L=2)": +6,
    "Label Attention":   0,
}

fig = go.Figure()
for arch, group in df.groupby("architecture"):
    offset = emb_offsets[arch]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["emb_dim"] + offset, y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="markers", name=arch, legendgroup=arch,
        marker=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), size=5, opacity=0.3),
        hovertemplate="emb_dim=%{customdata}<br>val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
        customdata=group["emb_dim"],
    ))
for arch, group in df_emb_mean.groupby("architecture"):
    offset = emb_offsets.get(arch, 0)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["emb_dim"] + offset, y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="lines+markers", name=arch, legendgroup=arch, showlegend=False,
        line=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), width=2), marker=dict(size=6),
        hovertemplate="emb_dim=%{customdata}<br>mean val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
        customdata=group["emb_dim"],
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Embedding dimension", yaxis_title="Validation loss",
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=emb_vals, ticktext=[str(v) for v in emb_vals]),
    legend_title="Architecture", height=430, width=750, margin=dict(l=60, r=20),
)
fig.show()
```

:::

**Vocabulary size.** All architectures benefit modestly from a larger vocabulary, but with diminishing returns after 10,000 tokens. FastText remains largely unaffected, barely improving from 0.952 at 2k to 0.940 at 20k. Transformer-based models show a clearer gain up to 10k, after which the curves flatten — suggesting that most of the useful vocabulary is captured within that range for this dataset.

**Embedding dimension.** The picture is similar: performance improves as embedding size grows from 64 to 256, but gains stall or even reverse at 512. Transformer (L=2) peaks at 256 (~0.873) then slightly degrades, suggesting that very large embeddings bring no benefit and may introduce optimization difficulties. FastText is again nearly insensitive to this parameter. For transformer architectures, **256 appears to be the sweet spot**.

### Effect of number of attention heads

```{python}
#| label: fig-nhead
#| fig-cap: "Effect of number of attention heads on validation loss"

df_attn = df[df["n_layers"] > 0].copy()
df_attn["n_head"] = df_attn["n_head"].astype(int)
head_vals = sorted(df_attn["n_head"].unique())

df_head_min = (
    df_attn.groupby(["architecture", "n_head"], as_index=False)["min_val_loss"].mean()
    .sort_values("n_head")
)

head_offsets = {
    "Transformer (L=1)": -0.075,
    "Transformer (L=2)": +0.075,
    "Label Attention":   0,
}

fig = go.Figure()
for arch, group in df_attn.groupby("architecture"):
    offset = head_offsets.get(arch, 0)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["n_head"] + offset, y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="markers", name=arch, legendgroup=arch,
        marker=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), size=5, opacity=0.3),
        hovertemplate="n_head=%{customdata}<br>val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
        customdata=group["n_head"],
    ))
for arch, group in df_head_min.groupby("architecture"):
    offset = head_offsets.get(arch, 0)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["n_head"] + offset, y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="lines+markers", name=arch, legendgroup=arch, showlegend=False,
        line=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), width=2), marker=dict(size=6),
        hovertemplate="n_head=%{customdata}<br>mean val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
        customdata=group["n_head"],
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Number of attention heads", yaxis_title="Validation loss",
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=head_vals, ticktext=[str(v) for v in head_vals]),
    legend_title="Architecture", height=420,
)
fig.show()
```

The number of attention heads has virtually no effect on validation loss across all three architectures. The mean curves are essentially flat from 2 to 8 heads, and the ranking between architectures remains unchanged. This is consistent with the theoretical expectation: as the total attention matrix size is fixed at $4 \times d^2$, splitting it into more heads simply redistributes capacity without adding parameters.je ne

### Training time

```{python}
#| label: fig-train-time
#| fig-cap: "Validation loss vs. training time"

fig = go.Figure()
for arch, group in df.groupby("architecture"):
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=group["train_time_s"], y=group["min_val_loss"],
        mode="markers", name=arch, legendgroup=arch,
        marker=dict(color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"), size=6, opacity=0.5),
        hovertemplate="time=%{x:.0f}s<br>val_loss=%{y:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Training time (s)", yaxis_title="Validation loss",
    legend_title="Architecture", height=430,
)
fig.show()
```

### Interaction: embedding dimension × vocabulary size

```{python}
#| label: fig-heatmap
#| fig-cap: "Mean validation loss by embedding dimension and vocabulary size"

from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

arch_order = ["FastText", "Transformer (L=1)", "Transformer (L=2)", "Label Attention"]
arch_order = [a for a in arch_order if a in df["architecture"].unique()]
emb_vals   = sorted(df["emb_dim"].unique())
vocab_vals  = sorted(df["vocab_size"].unique())

vmin = df.groupby(["architecture", "emb_dim", "vocab_size"])["min_val_loss"].mean().min()
vmax = df.groupby(["architecture", "emb_dim", "vocab_size"])["min_val_loss"].mean().max()

fig = make_subplots(rows=2, cols=2, subplot_titles=arch_order)

for idx, arch in enumerate(arch_order):
    row, col = divmod(idx, 2)
    pivot = (
        df[df["architecture"] == arch]
        .groupby(["emb_dim", "vocab_size"])["min_val_loss"].mean()
        .unstack("vocab_size")
        .reindex(index=emb_vals, columns=vocab_vals)
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Heatmap(
        z=pivot.values,
        x=[f"{v:,}" for v in vocab_vals],
        y=[str(v) for v in emb_vals],
        colorscale="Viridis_r",
        zmin=vmin, zmax=vmax,
        text=[[f"{v:.3f}" if not np.isnan(v) else "" for v in row_] for row_ in pivot.values],
        texttemplate="%{text}",
        showscale=(idx == 0),
        colorbar=dict(title="Mean val loss", len=0.45, y=0.78) if idx == 0 else None,
        hovertemplate="vocab=%{x}<br>emb_dim=%{y}<br>val_loss=%{z:.4f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
    ), row=row + 1, col=col + 1)
    fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Vocabulary size", row=row + 1, col=col + 1)
    fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Embedding dim", row=row + 1, col=col + 1)

fig.update_layout(height=600)
fig.show()
```

### Prediction analysis

```{python}
#| label: load-predictions-amazon

import numpy as np, tempfile, glob as glob_mod

STAR_LABELS  = ["1★", "2★", "3★", "4★", "5★"]
ARCH_SLUGS   = {
    "FastText":           "fasttext",
    "Transformer (L=1)": "transL1",
    "Transformer (L=2)": "transL2",
    "Label Attention":   "labatt",
}

preds_by_arch = {}   # arch -> {"y_test": ..., "y_pred": ..., "X_test": ...}

for arch, slug in ARCH_SLUGS.items():
    if arch not in df["architecture"].values:
        continue
    cache = Path(f"benchmark/results/predictions_amazon_{slug}.npz")
    if cache.exists():
        d = np.load(cache, allow_pickle=True)
        preds_by_arch[arch] = {
            "y_test": d["y_test"].astype(int),
            "y_pred": d["y_pred"].astype(int),
            "X_test": d["X_test"],
        }
    else:
        run_id = df[df["architecture"] == arch].loc[
            df[df["architecture"] == arch]["min_val_loss"].idxmin(), "run_id"
        ]
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            local_dir = mlflow.MlflowClient().download_artifacts(run_id, "predictions", dst_path=tmp)
            npz_file  = glob_mod.glob(f"{local_dir}/*.npz")[0]
            d         = np.load(npz_file, allow_pickle=True)
            preds_by_arch[arch] = {
                "y_test": d["y_test"].astype(int),
                "y_pred": d["y_pred"].astype(int),
                "X_test": d["X_test"],
            }
        np.savez(cache, **preds_by_arch[arch])

ARCHS_AVAIL = list(preds_by_arch.keys())
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Confusion matrix

```{python}
#| label: fig-confusion-amazon
#| fig-cap: "Confusion matrix on the Amazon test set"

from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix

n_archs = len(ARCHS_AVAIL)
fig = go.Figure()
for i, arch in enumerate(ARCHS_AVAIL):
    d  = preds_by_arch[arch]
    cm = confusion_matrix(d["y_test"], d["y_pred"], normalize="true")
    fig.add_trace(go.Heatmap(
        z=cm,
        x=STAR_LABELS, y=STAR_LABELS,
        colorscale="Blues", zmin=0, zmax=1,
        text=[[f"{v:.2f}" for v in row_] for row_ in cm],
        texttemplate="%{text}",
        colorbar=dict(title="Fraction"),
        hovertemplate="Pred=%{x}<br>True=%{y}<br>fraction=%{z:.3f}<extra></extra>",
        visible=(i == 0),
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Predicted", yaxis_title="True",
    yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"),
    height=500, width=540,
    margin=dict(t=125, r=20),
    updatemenus=[dict(
        type="buttons",
        direction="right",
        buttons=[
            dict(label=arch, method="update",
                 args=[{"visible": [j == i for j in range(n_archs)]}])
            for i, arch in enumerate(ARCHS_AVAIL)
        ],
        x=0, xanchor="left",
        y=1.18, yanchor="top",
        showactive=True,
        bgcolor="#f0f0f0",
        bordercolor="#cccccc",
        borderwidth=1,
        font=dict(size=12, color="#222"),
        pad=dict(l=4, r=4, t=4, b=4),
    )],
    annotations=[dict(
        text="<b>Choose the model:</b>", x=0, xanchor="left", xref="paper",
        y=1.21, yanchor="bottom", yref="paper",
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=12, color="#555"),
    )],
)
fig.show()
```

### Error analysis

```{python}
#| label: fig-error-seg-amazon
#| fig-cap: "Prediction distance per architecture"

import random
from collections import Counter
random.seed(42)

SEG_KEYS   = ["Correct", "Off by 1", "Off by 2", "Off by 3+"]
SEG_COLORS = {"Correct": "#43A047", "Off by 1": "#FDD835", "Off by 2": "#FB8C00", "Off by 3+": "#E53935"}
n_archs    = len(ARCHS_AVAIL)
labels_d   = [f"±{dd} star{'s' if dd>1 else ''}" for dd in range(1, 5)]

arch_segs, arch_bias = {}, {}
for arch in ARCHS_AVAIL:
    d        = preds_by_arch[arch]
    y_t, y_p = d["y_test"], d["y_pred"]
    dist     = np.abs(y_p - y_t)
    n        = len(dist)
    arch_segs[arch] = {
        "Correct":   (dist == 0).sum() / n * 100,
        "Off by 1":  (dist == 1).sum() / n * 100,
        "Off by 2":  (dist == 2).sum() / n * 100,
        "Off by 3+": (dist >= 3).sum() / n * 100,
    }
    signed = y_p - y_t
    n_tot  = len(signed)
    arch_bias[arch] = {
        "over":  [(signed ==  dd).sum() / n_tot * 100 for dd in range(1, 5)],
        "under": [(signed == -dd).sum() / n_tot * 100 for dd in range(1, 5)],
    }

fig1 = go.Figure()
for i, arch in enumerate(ARCHS_AVAIL):
    segs = arch_segs[arch]
    for seg in SEG_KEYS:
        pct = segs[seg]
        fig1.add_trace(go.Bar(
            x=[pct], y=[arch], orientation="h",
            name=seg, legendgroup=seg, showlegend=(i == 0),
            marker_color=SEG_COLORS[seg],
            text=f"<b>{seg}</b><br>{pct:.1f}%",
            textposition="inside", insidetextanchor="middle",
            textfont=dict(size=12, color="white" if seg != "Off by 1" else "#333"),
            hovertemplate=f"<b>{seg}</b>: {pct:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
        ))

fig1.update_layout(
    barmode="relative",
    template="simple_white",
    xaxis=dict(range=[0, 100], ticksuffix="%", showgrid=False, title_text="% of test examples"),
    yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", x=0.5, xanchor="center", y=-0.22, yanchor="top", itemclick=False, itemdoubleclick=False),
    height=360, margin=dict(t=20, b=80),
)
fig1.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-error-bias-amazon
#| fig-cap: "Directional bias per architecture"

fig2 = go.Figure()
for i, arch in enumerate(ARCHS_AVAIL):
    b = arch_bias[arch]
    fig2.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=[-u for u in b["under"]], y=labels_d,
        orientation="h", name="Under-predicted",
        legendgroup="under", showlegend=False,
        marker_color="#1565C0",
        text=[f"{u:.1f}%" for u in b["under"]],
        textposition="outside", textfont=dict(size=11, color="#333"), cliponaxis=False,
        hovertemplate="%{y} — under: <b>%{customdata:.1f}%</b><extra></extra>",
        customdata=b["under"],
        visible=(i == 0),
    ))
    fig2.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=b["over"], y=labels_d,
        orientation="h", name="Over-predicted",
        legendgroup="over", showlegend=False,
        marker_color="#E53935",
        text=[f"{v:.1f}%" for v in b["over"]],
        textposition="outside", textfont=dict(size=11, color="#333"), cliponaxis=False,
        hovertemplate="%{y} — over: <b>%{x:.1f}%</b><extra></extra>",
        visible=(i == 0),
    ))

# Traces fantômes permanents pour la légende (toujours visibles)
fig2.add_trace(go.Bar(x=[None], y=[None], orientation="h",
    name="Under-predicted", marker_color="#1565C0",
    legendgroup="under", showlegend=True))
fig2.add_trace(go.Bar(x=[None], y=[None], orientation="h",
    name="Over-predicted", marker_color="#E53935",
    legendgroup="over", showlegend=True))

def make_vis2(k):
    v = []
    for i in range(n_archs):
        v.extend([i == k, i == k])
    v.extend([True, True])  # traces fantômes toujours visibles
    return v

fig2.update_xaxes(
    tickvals=[-20, -15, -10, -5, 0, 5, 10, 15, 20],
    ticktext=["20%", "15%", "10%", "5%", "0", "5%", "10%", "15%", "20%"],
    range=[-22, 22],
    title_text="% of test examples",
)
fig2.update_yaxes(autorange="reversed")
fig2.add_vline(x=0, line_width=1.5, line_color="#333")

fig2.update_layout(
    barmode="relative",
    template="simple_white",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", x=0.5, xanchor="center", y=-0.18, yanchor="top", itemclick=False, itemdoubleclick=False),
    height=480, margin=dict(t=90, b=80),
    annotations=[dict(
        text="<b>Choose the model:</b>", x=0, xanchor="left", xref="paper",
        y=1.22, yanchor="bottom", yref="paper",
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=12, color="#555"),
    )],
    updatemenus=[dict(
        type="buttons", direction="right",
        buttons=[
            dict(label=arch, method="update", args=[{"visible": make_vis2(i)}])
            for i, arch in enumerate(ARCHS_AVAIL)
        ],
        x=0, xanchor="left", y=1.18, yanchor="top",
        showactive=True, bgcolor="#f0f0f0",
        bordercolor="#cccccc", borderwidth=1,
        font=dict(size=12, color="#222"),
        pad=dict(l=4, r=4, t=4, b=4),
    )],
)
fig2.show()
```

### Error overlap

```{python}
#| label: fig-error-overlap-jaccard
#| fig-cap: "Pairwise error overlap between architectures (Jaccard index)"

error_sets = {
    arch: set(np.where(preds_by_arch[arch]["y_pred"] != preds_by_arch[arch]["y_test"])[0])
    for arch in ARCHS_AVAIL
}
n_total = len(preds_by_arch[ARCHS_AVAIL[0]]["y_test"])
n = len(ARCHS_AVAIL)

jaccard = np.zeros((n, n))
overlap = np.zeros((n, n), dtype=int)
for i, a1 in enumerate(ARCHS_AVAIL):
    for j, a2 in enumerate(ARCHS_AVAIL):
        inter = len(error_sets[a1] & error_sets[a2])
        union = len(error_sets[a1] | error_sets[a2])
        jaccard[i, j] = inter / union if union > 0 else 1.0
        overlap[i, j] = inter

fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
    z=jaccard,
    x=ARCHS_AVAIL, y=ARCHS_AVAIL,
    colorscale="Blues", zmin=0, zmax=1,
    text=[[f"{jaccard[i,j]:.2f}<br>({overlap[i,j]:,})" for j in range(n)] for i in range(n)],
    texttemplate="%{text}",
    colorbar=dict(title="Jaccard"),
    hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b> ∩ <b>%{x}</b><br>Jaccard: %{z:.2f}<br>Shared errors: %{customdata:,}<extra></extra>",
    customdata=overlap,
))
fig.update_layout(
    yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"),
    height=380,
    margin=dict(t=20),
)
fig.show()
```

### Error patterns

```{python}
#| label: fig-delta-confusion
#| fig-cap: "Δ confusion matrix vs FastText — green = improvement, red = regression"

from plotly.subplots import make_subplots as _make_subplots

if "FastText" in preds_by_arch:
    cm_ref   = confusion_matrix(preds_by_arch["FastText"]["y_test"],
                                preds_by_arch["FastText"]["y_pred"], normalize="true")
    cmp_archs = [a for a in ARCHS_AVAIL if a != "FastText"]
    fig = _make_subplots(rows=1, cols=len(cmp_archs), subplot_titles=cmp_archs)

    for idx, arch in enumerate(cmp_archs):
        cm    = confusion_matrix(preds_by_arch[arch]["y_test"],
                                 preds_by_arch[arch]["y_pred"], normalize="true")
        delta = cm - cm_ref
        fig.add_trace(go.Heatmap(
            z=delta, x=STAR_LABELS, y=STAR_LABELS,
            colorscale="RdYlGn", zmid=0,
            text=[[f"{v:+.2f}" for v in row_] for row_ in delta],
            texttemplate="%{text}",
            showscale=(idx == 0),
            colorbar=dict(title="Δ", len=0.8) if idx == 0 else None,
            hovertemplate="Pred=%{x}<br>True=%{y}<br>Δ=%{z:+.3f}<extra>" + arch + "</extra>",
        ), row=1, col=idx + 1)
        fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Predicted",                  row=1, col=idx + 1)
        fig.update_yaxes(title_text="True" if idx == 0 else "",
                         autorange="reversed",                     row=1, col=idx + 1)

    fig.update_layout(height=430)
    fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-per-class-accuracy
#| fig-cap: "Per-class recall by architecture"

fig = go.Figure()
for arch in ARCHS_AVAIL:
    d   = preds_by_arch[arch]
    cm  = confusion_matrix(d["y_test"], d["y_pred"], normalize="true")
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=STAR_LABELS, y=np.diag(cm),
        name=arch, marker_color=COLORS.get(arch, "grey"),
        hovertemplate=f"<b>{arch}</b><br>%{{x}}: %{{y:.1%}}<extra></extra>",
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    yaxis_title="Recall", yaxis_tickformat=".0%",
    legend_title="Architecture",
    template="simple_white", height=400,
)
fig.show()
```

### Examples

```{python}
#| label: fig-examples-amazon
#| fig-cap: "Example predictions on the Amazon test set"

import json, random
from IPython.display import HTML

random.seed(42)

y_test = preds_by_arch[ARCHS_AVAIL[0]]["y_test"]
X_test = preds_by_arch[ARCHS_AVAIL[0]]["X_test"]
texts  = X_test.tolist() if X_test.ndim == 1 else X_test[:, 0].tolist()

df_ex = pd.DataFrame({"true": y_test, "text": texts})
for arch in ARCHS_AVAIL:
    df_ex[arch] = preds_by_arch[arch]["y_pred"]

df_ex["disagree"] = df_ex[ARCHS_AVAIL].nunique(axis=1) > 1
df_ex["any_wrong"] = (df_ex[ARCHS_AVAIL].values != df_ex[["true"]].values).any(axis=1)
pool   = df_ex[df_ex["disagree"] & df_ex["any_wrong"]]
sample = pool.sample(12, random_state=42).reset_index(drop=True)

ARCH_SHORT = {"FastText": "FastText", "Transformer (L=1)": "Trans (L=1)",
              "Transformer (L=2)": "Trans (L=2)", "Label Attention": "Label Att"}

examples = [
    {"true": int(r["true"]), "text": str(r["text"]),
     "preds": {a: int(r[a]) for a in ARCHS_AVAIL}}
    for _, r in sample.iterrows()
]

html = f"""
<style>
  .ex-wrap  {{ font-family:sans-serif; font-size:14px; max-width:100%; }}
  .ex-card  {{ border:1px solid #ddd; border-radius:8px; padding:20px 24px;
               background:#fafafa; margin-bottom:14px; }}
  .ex-meta  {{ font-size:13px; color:#555; margin-bottom:10px; }}
  .ex-meta b {{ color:#222; }}
  .ex-text  {{ line-height:1.75; color:#222; margin-bottom:18px; }}
  .ex-preds {{ display:flex; gap:12px; flex-wrap:wrap; }}
  .ex-model {{ display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center; gap:4px; }}
  .ex-mname {{ font-size:11px; color:#888; font-weight:600; text-transform:uppercase;
               letter-spacing:.04em; }}
  .ex-badge {{ padding:5px 14px; border-radius:5px; font-weight:700; font-size:15px; }}
  .correct  {{ background:#d4edda; color:#155724; }}
  .wrong    {{ background:#f8d7da; color:#721c24; }}
  .ex-nav   {{ display:flex; align-items:center; gap:12px; }}
  .ex-btn   {{ padding:6px 18px; background:#f0f0f0; border:1px solid #ccc;
               border-radius:5px; cursor:pointer; font-size:13px; }}
  .ex-btn:hover {{ background:#e0e0e0; }}
  .ex-count {{ font-size:12px; color:#999; }}
</style>

<div class="ex-wrap">
  <div class="ex-nav" style="justify-content:center; margin-bottom:12px;">
    <button class="ex-btn" onclick="exNav(-1)">← Prev</button>
    <span class="ex-count" id="ex-count"></span>
    <button class="ex-btn" onclick="exNav(1)">Next →</button>
  </div>
  <div class="ex-card">
    <div class="ex-meta" id="ex-meta"></div>
    <div class="ex-text" id="ex-text"></div>
    <div class="ex-preds" id="ex-preds"></div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function() {{
  const EX   = {json.dumps(examples)};
  const SL   = {json.dumps(STAR_LABELS)};
  const AS   = {json.dumps(ARCH_SHORT)};
  let cur = 0;

  function render(i) {{
    const e = EX[i];
    document.getElementById('ex-meta').innerHTML =
      'True rating: <b>' + SL[e.true] + '</b>';
    document.getElementById('ex-text').textContent = e.text;
    let h = '';
    for (const [arch, pred] of Object.entries(e.preds)) {{
      const cls = pred === e.true ? 'correct' : 'wrong';
      h += '<div class="ex-model"><span class="ex-mname">' + (AS[arch]||arch) + '</span>'
         + '<span class="ex-badge ' + cls + '">' + SL[pred] + '</span></div>';
    }}
    document.getElementById('ex-preds').innerHTML = h;
    document.getElementById('ex-count').textContent = (i+1) + ' / ' + EX.length;
  }}

  window.exNav = function(d) {{ cur = (cur + d + EX.length) % EX.length; render(cur); }};
  render(0);
}})();
</script>
"""
HTML(html)
```

:::

## IMDb

*Coming soon.*

:::


## Explainability

This section compares how two architectures — **Label Attention** (best model) and its **mean-pooling counterpart** (same backbone, no label attention) — respond to the same test examples, using Captum Layer Integrated Gradients attributions produced by `benchmark/explain.py`.

Two complementary lenses are presented:

- **Corpus-level word importance**: attributions aggregated over 200 test examples, surfacing the vocabulary each star rating relies on.
- **Per-example token attribution**: for individual reviews, signed Captum scores show which words push each model toward or away from each class — side by side for direct comparison.

```{python}
#| label: load-explain-artifacts

import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from collections import defaultdict

import mlflow
import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
```
