```{python}
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

EXPLAIN_RUN_LABATT  = "8286dc715ad54791bbb1407d840d528b"
EXPLAIN_RUN_POOLING = "???"   # replace with pooling model run ID once available

_CANDIDATE_NAMES = ["captum.npz", "captum_attn.npz"]   # try new name, fall back to old

def _load_captum(run_id: str):
    """Download captum artifact for run_id; returns None if unavailable."""
    if not run_id or run_id.startswith("?"):
        return None
    cache_dir = Path(f"benchmark/results/explain_{run_id[:8]}")
    cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    # Return from cache — check all candidate filenames
    for name in _CANDIDATE_NAMES:
        cache = cache_dir / name
        if cache.exists():
            return np.load(cache, allow_pickle=True)
    # Try downloading from MLflow
    client = mlflow.MlflowClient()
    for name in _CANDIDATE_NAMES:
        try:
            with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
                local = client.download_artifacts(run_id, f"explainability/{name}", dst_path=tmp)
                d     = np.load(local, allow_pickle=True)
                cache = cache_dir / "captum.npz"
                np.savez(str(cache), **{k: d[k] for k in d.files})
                return np.load(cache, allow_pickle=True)
        except Exception:
            continue
    return None

cap_la = _load_captum(EXPLAIN_RUN_LABATT)
cap_pl = _load_captum(EXPLAIN_RUN_POOLING)

HAS_LA = cap_la is not None
HAS_PL = cap_pl is not None
HAS_BOTH = HAS_LA and HAS_PL

if not HAS_LA:
    print("Label Attention artifacts not yet available — re-run benchmark/explain.py")
else:
    N_CLS   = cap_la["word_attn"][0].shape[0]
    STARS   = [f"{i+1}★" for i in range(N_CLS)]
    PALETTE = ["#1565C0", "#2E7D32", "#F57F17", "#6A1B9A", "#B71C1C"]

    MODEL_DATA   = {"labatt": cap_la}
    MODEL_LABELS = {"labatt": "Label Attention"}
    if HAS_PL:
        MODEL_DATA["pooling"]   = cap_pl
        MODEL_LABELS["pooling"] = "Mean Pooling"

    def _class_sorted(cap, i: int) -> np.ndarray:
        inv = np.argsort(cap["class_order"][i])
        return cap["word_attn"][i][inv]
```

### Corpus-level word importance

```{python}
#| label: fig-captum-corpus
#| fig-cap: "Top words per star rating (Captum IG, mean over 200 examples, min. 5 occurrences). Bars in class colour push toward the class; red bars push away."

if not HAS_LA:
    print("Artifacts not yet available.")
else:
    def _corpus_acc(cap) -> list:
        acc = [defaultdict(list) for _ in range(N_CLS)]
        for i, wds in enumerate(cap["words"]):
            mat = _class_sorted(cap, i)
            for c in range(N_CLS):
                for j, w in enumerate(list(wds)):
                    if j < mat.shape[1]:
                        acc[c][w.lower()].append(float(mat[c, j]))
        return acc

    TOP_K = 12

    def _corpus_traces(acc: list) -> list:
        traces = []
        for c in range(N_CLS):
            means = {w: float(np.mean(v)) for w, v in acc[c].items() if len(v) >= 5}
            top   = sorted(means.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:TOP_K]
            ws    = [w for w, _ in top][::-1]
            ss    = [float(s) for _, s in top][::-1]
            traces.append(go.Bar(
                x=ss, y=ws, orientation="h",
                marker_color=[PALETTE[c] if s >= 0 else "#ef5350" for s in ss],
                showlegend=False,
                hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.4f}<extra></extra>",
            ))
        return traces

    accs_per_model = {k: _corpus_acc(v) for k, v in MODEL_DATA.items()}
    traces_per_model = {k: _corpus_traces(v) for k, v in accs_per_model.items()}
    n_tr = N_CLS

    fig_corp = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=N_CLS, subplot_titles=STARS, shared_yaxes=False)

    for model_key, traces in traces_per_model.items():
        for c, tr in enumerate(traces):
            fig_corp.add_trace(tr, row=1, col=c + 1)
        fig_corp.update_xaxes(
            showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee",
            zeroline=True, zerolinecolor="#bbb",
            title_text="Mean IG score",
            row=1, col=c + 1,
        )

    # Default: show only first model (labatt); hide others
    n_models = len(traces_per_model)
    for i, tr in enumerate(fig_corp.data):
        tr.visible = (i < n_tr)   # first n_tr traces = labatt

    layout_extra = {}
    if HAS_BOTH:
        vis_la = [True]  * n_tr + [False] * n_tr
        vis_pl = [False] * n_tr + [True]  * n_tr
        layout_extra = dict(
            annotations=[dict(
                text="<b>Model:</b>", x=0, xanchor="left", xref="paper",
                y=1.18, yanchor="bottom", yref="paper",
                showarrow=False, font=dict(size=12, color="#555"),
            )],
            updatemenus=[dict(
                type="buttons", direction="right",
                buttons=[
                    dict(label="Label Attention", method="restyle",
                         args=[{"visible": vis_la}]),
                    dict(label="Mean Pooling",    method="restyle",
                         args=[{"visible": vis_pl}]),
                ],
                x=0.12, xanchor="left", y=1.15, yanchor="top",
                showactive=True, bgcolor="#f0f0f0",
                bordercolor="#ccc", borderwidth=1,
                font=dict(size=12, color="#222"),
                pad=dict(l=4, r=4, t=4, b=4),
            )],
        )

    fig_corp.update_layout(
        height=440, margin=dict(t=100 if HAS_BOTH else 50, b=20, l=10, r=10),
        paper_bgcolor="white", plot_bgcolor="white",
        **layout_extra,
    )
    fig_corp.show()
```

### Per-example token attribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-captum-example
#| fig-cap: "Captum IG attributions for individual reviews. Each row = one star class. Blue = pushes toward the class; red = pushes away. Navigate with the numbered buttons."

if not HAS_LA:
    print("Artifacts not yet available.")
else:
    MAX_WORDS = 30
    N_EX_SHOW = 10
    N_COLS    = 2 if HAS_BOTH else 1
    col_titles = ["Label Attention", "Mean Pooling"] if HAS_BOTH else ["Label Attention"]

    y_true_la = cap_la["y_true"].astype(int)
    y_pred_la = cap_la["y_pred"].astype(int)
    rng       = np.random.default_rng(42)

    ex_indices: list = []
    for c in range(N_CLS):
        mask = np.where((y_true_la == c) & (y_pred_la == c))[0]
        if len(mask):
            ex_indices.append(int(rng.choice(mask)))
    remaining = [i for i in np.where(y_pred_la == y_true_la)[0] if i not in ex_indices]
    rng.shuffle(remaining)
    ex_indices += list(remaining[: N_EX_SHOW - len(ex_indices)])
    ex_indices  = ex_indices[:N_EX_SHOW]

    def _ex_arrays(cap, i: int):
        wds  = list(cap["words"][i])[:MAX_WORDS]
        mat  = _class_sorted(cap, i)[:, :len(wds)]
        amax = float(np.abs(mat).max()) or 1.0
        return wds, mat, amax

    payloads = []
    for ei in ex_indices:
        wds_la, mat_la, amax_la = _ex_arrays(cap_la, ei)
        entry = {
            "title": (
                f"y_true={STARS[y_true_la[ei]]}  —  "
                f"\"{str(cap_la['texts'][ei])[:100]}…\""
            ),
            "wds_la": wds_la, "mat_la": mat_la, "amax_la": amax_la,
        }
        if HAS_BOTH:
            wds_pl, mat_pl, amax_pl = _ex_arrays(cap_pl, ei)
            entry.update({"wds_pl": wds_pl, "mat_pl": mat_pl, "amax_pl": amax_pl})
        payloads.append(entry)

    fig = make_subplots(
        rows=N_CLS, cols=N_COLS,
        column_titles=col_titles,
        vertical_spacing=0.05,
        horizontal_spacing=0.06,
    )

    col_specs = [("wds_la", "mat_la", "amax_la")]
    if HAS_BOTH:
        col_specs.append(("wds_pl", "mat_pl", "amax_pl"))

    init = payloads[0]
    for c in range(N_CLS):
        for col_idx, (wds_key, mat_key, amax_key) in enumerate(col_specs):
            wds  = init[wds_key]
            sc   = init[mat_key][c].tolist()
            amax = init[amax_key]
            fig.add_trace(
                go.Bar(
                    x=list(range(len(wds))), y=sc,
                    marker=dict(
                        color=sc, colorscale="RdBu", cmid=0,
                        cmin=-amax, cmax=amax, showscale=False,
                    ),
                    text=wds, textposition="outside",
                    hovertemplate="%{text}: %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
                    showlegend=False,
                ),
                row=c + 1, col=col_idx + 1,
            )
            fig.update_xaxes(showticklabels=False, row=c + 1, col=col_idx + 1)
            fig.update_yaxes(
                zeroline=True, zerolinecolor="#bbb",
                title_text=STARS[c] if col_idx == 0 else "",
                row=c + 1, col=col_idx + 1,
            )

    n_traces = N_COLS * N_CLS
    nav_buttons = []
    for ei_idx, payload in enumerate(payloads):
        new_x, new_y, new_text = [], [], []
        for c in range(N_CLS):
            for wds_key, mat_key, _amax_key in col_specs:
                wds = payload[wds_key]
                sc  = payload[mat_key][c].tolist()
                new_x.append(list(range(len(wds))))
                new_y.append(sc)
                new_text.append(wds)
        nav_buttons.append(dict(
            label=str(ei_idx + 1),
            method="update",
            args=[
                {"x": new_x, "y": new_y, "text": new_text},
                {"title.text": payload["title"]},
            ],
        ))

    fig.update_layout(
        height=150 * N_CLS,
        title_text=init["title"],
        title_font_size=11,
        margin=dict(t=110, b=20, l=60, r=20),
        paper_bgcolor="white", plot_bgcolor="white",
        annotations=[dict(
            text="<b>Example:</b>", x=0, xanchor="left", xref="paper",
            y=1.09, yanchor="bottom", yref="paper",
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=12, color="#555"),
        )],
        updatemenus=[dict(
            type="buttons", direction="right",
            buttons=nav_buttons,
            x=0.10, xanchor="left", y=1.06, yanchor="top",
            showactive=True, bgcolor="#f0f0f0",
            bordercolor="#ccc", borderwidth=1,
            font=dict(size=12, color="#222"),
            pad=dict(l=4, r=4, t=4, b=4),
        )],
    )
    fig.show()
```
